description: Live detection dashboard for the DeSFAM ML inference engine. Monitors per-process syscall sequences streamed from eBPF (Tetragon) and scores them with an Isolation Forest + VAE ensemble. Threshold derived from validation-set percentile; scores above it trigger alerts.

panels.title: Guide — How to read this dashboard (click to expand)

options.content:
## DeSFAM — Syscall Anomaly Detection Dashboard

**How scoring works:** Tetragon (eBPF) captures every syscall made by each container. When a process exits, its full syscall trace is scored by two models and combined into one ensemble score.

| Component | What it measures | High score means |
|---|---|---|
| **Isolation Forest (IF)** | Unusual syscall *frequency* — anomalies isolated in fewer tree splits | Call frequencies are far from normal |
| **VAE (Autoencoder)** | Unusual syscall *sequence patterns* — trained on normal traces only | Sequence structure doesn't match normal behavior |
| **Ensemble** | `0.7 × RobustScaled(VAE error) + 0.3 × RobustScaled(IF score)` | Combined anomaly signal |

**Why scores can be negative:** Both scalers are centred on the *median* of normal training scores, so a typical normal trace scores near 0. Traces scoring below 0 are more-normal-than-average.

**Threshold (0.0197):** The model was trained on DongTing cluster data. This cluster's normal baseline differs slightly — live calibration showed normal pods score ≈ 0.05–0.06. Threshold is set to 0.0197 (the 99th-percentile of live dev-cluster scores during 2-minute calibration). Most pods score near `-0.06`; only the top 1% breach 0.02. Genuine attacks should score ≥ 0.05.

**Why the IF score is negative for normal pods:** sklearn's `decision_function` returns **positive** for normal samples (long path through trees = not easily isolated) and **negative** for anomalies (short path = isolated quickly). We negate it so that *higher always means more anomalous*. A healthy pod shows IF ≈ −0.10 (normal); an attacking pod shows IF > 0 (anomalous).

> **Quick investigation guide:** High consecutive attacks → sustained, not a false positive. Check **Last Attack Trace → Top Syscalls** for behavioural evidence (e.g. `mmap×1000` = memory abuse · `connect×100` = port scan · `openat×50` = file snooping).

| Panel | Meaning |
|---|---|
| **Total Attack Events** | Cumulative attack-classified traces since detector start. Resets on restart. Filter by Pod (top of page) to see per-pod totals. |
| **Pods in Stream** | Pods sending syscall events via Tetragon. Appears immediately on first event — before any scoring. |
| **Pods Scored** | Pods with at least one length-15 window scored. Lower than "in stream" if some processes haven't exited yet. |
| **Detection Threshold** | Ensemble score cut-off: score ≥ threshold → ATTACK. Raise to reduce false positives; lower to catch more attacks. |
| **p95 Latency** | 95th-percentile time to score one trace (featurize + IF + VAE). Under 50 ms is healthy for the current event rate. |

Each data point represents one **length-15 syscall window** (stride 3, per-PID). With 12 of 15 syscalls overlapping between adjacent windows, a single long-running process generates many overlapping data points. The **dashed orange line** is the detection threshold — traces above it are classified as attacks. Score = `0.7 × VAE_scaled + 0.3 × IF_scaled`. After scaling, the centre of normal behaviour is near 0; attack traces score higher. Spikes near or above the threshold line are worth investigating — check **Last Attack Trace** for the syscall breakdown.

| Panel | Meaning |
|---|---|
| **Attack Rate %** | Percentage of the last 20 scored traces classified as attacks. Near 100% = pod is *consistently* anomalous, not a one-off spike. |
| **Consecutive Attack Windows** | Back-to-back attack traces with no normal trace in between. Resets to 0 on the first normal trace. A large count is the strongest attack signal — sustained anomaly across multiple process executions. |

**Why the Isolation Forest score is negative for normal pods:**
sklearn's `decision_function` returns **positive** for normal samples (long path = many splits needed = not easily isolated) and **negative** for anomalies (short path = isolated quickly). We negate it so higher always means more anomalous. Normal pods show IF ≈ −0.10; attacking pods show IF > 0.

**Compare the two models to understand *why* a trace was flagged:**
- **VAE high + IF low (negative)** → unusual syscall *sequence* (call order / bigrams atypical, e.g. unexpected execve after socket)
- **IF high (positive) + VAE low** → unusual syscall *frequency* (one call dominates far more than normal, e.g. mmap ×1000)
- **Both high** → strongest attack signal — both frequency and sequence are anomalous

| Panel | Meaning |
|---|---|
| **Windows Scored / min** | Length-15 syscall windows scored per minute. With stride 3, every active PID emits ~1 window per 3 syscalls — busy pods (network proxies, web servers) score thousands per minute; idle pods score zero. |
| **Inference Latency** | p50 / p95 / p99 scoring time per trace. Spikes in p99 indicate bursty PIDs (many windows emitted in one batch) or CPU contention on the host. Under 50 ms p95 is healthy. |

panels.title: Overview
panels.description: High-level health of the DeSFAM detector. Use these tiles to get an instant read on whether attacks are being detected and whether the detector is running normally.

panels.title: Confirmed Attacks (this view)
panels.description: Count of attack flags within the currently selected dashboard time range. Cumulative `desfam_attacks_total` includes flags from earlier runs with stale thresholds, so the range-windowed count is more useful.

panels.title: Pods Under Attack
panels.description: Pods with rolling attack rate above 50 % — meaning more than half of their recent process traces scored above the detection threshold. This is the primary triage signal: zero means quiet; non-zero means investigate immediately.

panels.title: Pods in Stream
panels.description: Number of distinct pods currently sending syscall events to the detector via Tetragon gRPC. A pod appears here as soon as its first syscall event arrives — before any scoring occurs. If a pod you expect is missing, check that Tetragon is running on its node and the TracingPolicy is applied.

panels.title: Detection Threshold
panels.description: The ensemble score boundary used to classify a length-15 syscall window: score ≥ threshold → ATTACK, score < threshold → normal. The score is 0.7 × RobustScaled(VAE reconstruction error) + 0.3 × RobustScaled(Isolation Forest score). After scaling, typical normal windows cluster near zero; attack windows score higher. The threshold (0.0197) was calibrated against 2 minutes of live dev-cluster traffic and is the 99th-percentile of observed prod scores — only the top 1% triggers alerts. Raise it to reduce false positives; lower it to catch more anomalies.

panels.title: p95 Scoring Latency
panels.description: 95th-percentile time (in milliseconds) to score one completed process trace: featurize (build 149-dim vector) + Isolation Forest predict + VAE encode/decode. p95 = 95% of traces are scored within this time. Under 50 ms is healthy for the current event rate. If this grows consistently above 200 ms the detector may fall behind the live Tetragon stream.

panels.title: Current Anomaly Risk Ranking
panels.description: Real-time anomaly score per pod, sorted by severity. Gradient: green (normal) → yellow (near threshold) → orange (HIGH) → red (CRIT). Scores are the IF+VAE ensemble output; positive = anomalous.

panels.title: Anomaly Scores
panels.description: Per-pod ensemble anomaly scores over time. Each point represents one scored process trace. The dashed orange line is the detection threshold — traces above it are classified as attacks.

panels.title: Ensemble Score per Pod
panels.description: IF+VAE ensemble score per pod over time. Orange dashed line = detection threshold (from validation set). Shaded zone above threshold = alert region. Pure socket-I/O pods (recvfrom/write/close) score HIGH due to domain shift — cross-check "Files Accessed" in the triage table before escalating.

panels.title: Attack Detection
panels.description: Derived attack signals: rolling attack rate and consecutive-window filter. Use these to distinguish sustained attacks from brief spikes.

panels.title: Pod Attack State History
panels.description: Per-pod attack state over time. Red = confirmed attack window (ensemble score > threshold, consecutive ≥ configured minimum). Use this to spot sustained attacks vs. isolated spikes.

panels.title: Component Scores
panels.description: Individual scores from the two models that feed the ensemble. Useful for diagnosing why the ensemble score is high: is it driven by reconstruction error (VAE), path-length anomaly (IF), or both?

panels.title: VAE Reconstruction Error
panels.description: Mean-squared reconstruction error from the Variational Autoencoder. Trained exclusively on normal DongTing kernel-fuzzing sequences. High error → sequence structure deviates from training distribution. Values >0.5 on .NET/nginx workloads indicate domain/covariate shift (likely FP), not necessarily a real attack.

panels.title: Isolation Forest Score
panels.description: Isolation Forest anomaly score (negative: more negative = more isolated = more anomalous). Trained on normal sequences only (contamination=0.02, 300 trees). Less sensitive to domain shift than VAE — uses relative depth, not absolute reconstruction. Weighted 30% in the ensemble (α=0.7 for VAE).

panels.title: Performance
panels.description: Detector throughput and latency. Use this section to verify the detector is keeping up with the event rate and to spot performance regressions after config changes.

panels.title: Windows Scored (per minute)
panels.description: Rate of complete syscall windows scored per minute. A window is emitted on process exit, max-length hit, or idle timeout.

panels.title: Syscall Events / min
panels.description: Raw syscall events ingested from Tetragon gRPC stream per minute. Spike = high-activity process (e.g., bulk I/O, exec burst).

panels.title: Inference Latency Percentiles
panels.description: ML inference latency distribution: feature extraction + IF score + VAE encode/decode. p95 < 50ms is acceptable for real-time detection.

panels.title: Attack Sequences
panels.description: Syscall-level evidence for confirmed attacks. Shows what the model actually saw inside the anomalous process trace.

panels.title: Active threshold T over time (ensemble-score space)
panels.description: Solid red = current T used to decide flag vs no-flag (ensemble anomaly score). Dashed green = the model's trained T₀=p995. Dashed grey = EMA_TMIN floor. T moves between these only on non-flagged windows (paper §IV.B.3 Algorithm 2 line 16). The per-model VAE / IF thresholds live in different score spaces and are shown separately in the panels above.

panels.title: Last Attack — Triage summary (pod / severity / syscall trace)
panels.description: Per-pod summary of the most recent confirmed attack: severity bucket, top syscalls, the first 12 deduped calls of the offending window, file paths and binaries it touched. Pairs with the feature-vector table below — *what* the model saw vs *where* the alert came from.

panels.title: Attack history — last 20 windows the model flagged (newest first, 15m)
panels.description: Each row = one attack-flagged window the model evaluated. Columns 2–7 = the six paper_plus feature groups; each cell holds the dim=value list (only dims with |scaled| ≥ 0.5, sorted by deviation, capped at 8 per group + '(+N more)'). Score column = ensemble anomaly score for that window. Rolling buffer of 20 attacks per pod; old ones are evicted when newer attacks land.

panels.title: VAE T₀ — anomaly recon error scale
panels.description: VAE reconstruction-error space (NOT comparable to IF/ensemble).

panels.title: IF T₀ — isolation-depth score scale
panels.description: Isolation-Forest score space (NOT comparable to VAE/ensemble).

panels.title: Ensemble T₀ — final-decision scale (this drives the flag)
panels.description: Post-fusion ensemble score — what is actually compared to `desfam_threshold` at runtime.

panels.title: EMA calculator — how T is computed (paper §IV.B.3 Eq. 3)

options.content:
## The equation

```
T_{t+1}   =   max(   T_min   ,   β · T_t   +   (1 − β) · A(W_i)   )
```

Applied **only on non-flagged windows** (where A ≤ T_t). Attack scores skip the update, so T cannot drift upward and silence subsequent detections — paper §IV.B.3 Algorithm 2 line 16.

### Read the eight cards below left-to-right
1–4 = the **inputs** (live: T_t, β, A(W_i), T_min) · 5–7 = the **intermediate products** · 8 = the **result** that became `desfam_threshold`.

panels.title: Tetragon syscall events — ns demo
panels.description: Live Tetragon syscall-event stream (export-stdout) filtered to ns demo pods. Source: Loki, scraped by Promtail from the Tetragon node log.

panels.title: Tetragon syscall events — all (raw)
panels.description: Full Tetragon export-stdout event stream (all namespaces the policy traces).

panels.title: ns demo — workload logs
panels.description: Application stdout/stderr of the demo workloads (per-pod label `pod`).

title: DeSFAM — Runtime Syscall Anomaly Detection
